patterns.description: Eval 3 (refactoring judgment) passes 3/3 for both configs. Sonnet produces identical quality refactoring advice with or without the skill. Same result as iteration 1 with Opus.
patterns.recommendation: Eval 3 assertions do not measure skill value-add. Consider adding assertions that test for skill-specific behaviors like boundary parsing recommendations or confidence-calibrated judgment.

patterns.description: Eval 0 with-skill dropped from 5/5 (Opus) to 3/5 (Sonnet). Sonnet failed to use as const derivation and specific return type union despite the skill explicitly teaching these patterns.
patterns.recommendation: The skill's instructions on as const derivation and return type specificity may need stronger emphasis or worked examples to land with smaller models.

patterns.description: Eval 2 with-skill dropped from 6/6 (Opus, original assertions) to 6/9 (Sonnet, expanded assertions). Three new assertions failed: parallel type drift, confidence framework, standard section mapping. Sonnet ignored the confidence framework and section mapping entirely.
patterns.recommendation: The confidence framework and section mapping are structural output requirements. The skill may need to make these output format requirements more explicit, possibly with a template.

patterns.description: Eval 1 (API client) remains the strongest discriminator: 4/4 with skill vs 1/4 without. Boundary parsing with Zod is a clear capability uplift that Sonnet does not produce unprompted.
patterns.recommendation: This eval reliably measures the skill's core value proposition across both Opus and Sonnet.

patterns.description: Sonnet uses 33% fewer tokens on average (14.3K vs estimated Opus baseline) and runs faster (38.5s vs estimated Opus). The without-skill baseline is significantly cheaper (10.8K tokens, 20.9s).
patterns.recommendation: Token overhead from skill loading is proportionally larger on Sonnet. The skill adds ~32% token cost for ~36.5% pass rate improvement.

observations: Opus outperforms Sonnet both with and without the skill
The skill provides larger absolute uplift on Sonnet (+36.5%) vs Opus (+41.6%), but Sonnet starts from a lower baseline
Eval 1 (boundary parsing) is the most stable discriminator across models
Eval 3 (refactoring) is non-discriminating across both models
Eval 0 shows the largest model gap: Opus with-skill achieves 5/5, Sonnet with-skill only 3/5
